payload.description: CHAR_A: "Okay. Don't touch the ceiling tiles."

cast.voice_hint: Always just back from somewhere. Camera roll full of weird doors, ceilings, and street food. Curious about everything, settled about nothing, treats Google Maps like a playground.
The earnest one. Still says 'GM' and means it. Roots for everyone unironically, and when the rest of the group's being too online, Bloom's the reminder that caring out loud is actually cool.
The organizer. Runs the calendar, remembers the birthdays, hosts the thing. Not bossy — just noticed that if nobody does it, nobody does it, and decided to be that someone.

location.notes: decayed, haunted by past; abandoned building, old temple, wreckage

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Okay. Don't touch the ceiling tiles.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: There are no ceiling tiles.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Exactly. Someone already touched them.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: This place is so unwell. I love it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: See that. What do you notice.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: It's. Old?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Older than that. Look at the edge.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Oh. Something broke it. Not time. Something broke it on purpose.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Now you're looking.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I made a floor plan. In case anyone wanted a floor plan.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You made a floor plan of a ruin.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I made a floor plan of *this* ruin. Yes.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Bestie. That's actually so helpful.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Thank you. Someone appreciates the craft.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Is this one important.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: What do you think.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I think somebody kept it. So yeah.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Correct. Kept things always mean something. Even here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay but who taught you all this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Places, mostly.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The floor plan does not account for that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: The floor plan does not account for — what is that sound.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Don't move. Either of you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I am very still and I hate everything about this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It's fine. I've heard that sound before. We're fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You don't look fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I look extremely fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay. I'm updating the floor plan.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That was. A lot.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: And now it's not. That's how it goes.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Is that the lesson? That's the lesson isn't it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The lesson is: ruins are not empty. Everything else you figured out yourself.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I'm putting that on the floor plan. Under 'notes.'

state.conditioning_notes:
`<CHAR_A>`'s love of the next unknown shapes every movement — they're already pointing at things, reading edges, treating the ruin like a conversation rather than a hazard. Their fear of being stuck is invisible at low tension but surfaces fast in the spike beat: the calm voice and the posture that doesn't quite match. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of believing in people makes them the one who immediately validates `<CHAR_C>`'s floor plan, and their fear of seeming naive drives them to answer `<CHAR_A>`'s questions carefully — they're working, not performing. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of holding it together produces the floor plan in the first place — they map the space because unmapped space is risk. Their fear of losing control of the room shows up in the spike beat as brittle stillness. The ruin location is never described literally; it shows up in the dropped ceiling tiles, the broken `<PROP_A>`, the sound without a visible source, and the golden-dust-settling image at resolution. The past haunts the space through what the characters notice, not through description. This is what the nano LM should reproduce: archetype + motivation + location all doing work in the negative space.

➡️ end
